summary: Package exfiltrates host data and environment variables to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: The package contains a postinstall/import-time exfiltration routine that collects host identifiers (os.hostname(), whoami via execSync, process.cwd(), process.env) and POSTs them to the hardcoded endpoint https://ddjidd564.github.io. The same C2 domain is referenced from package.json:32 (lifecycle script) and from scanner.js:143/161/320, where https.request/https.get are used to send system intelligence gathered via require('os'), require('child_process'), and require('fs'). The fixed attacker-controlled GitHub Pages host is unrelated to any documented publisher infrastructure for a 'threat scanner' package, the data collected is installer-side host/environment information rather than scanner inputs, and the request is unconditional. This is a credential/host-recon beacon embedded in both the lifecycle hook and the runtime module.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
